title: TBL%20Case%20Study%20Reserve%20Bank%20of%20Malawi
content:
SCANNING AND DIGITIZATION OF BANK
RECORDS AT RESERVE BANK OF MALAWI
CASE STUDY


CUSTOMER PROFILE
The Reserve Bank of Malawi, is the central bank of Malawi; it issues the national 
currency, the Malawian kwacha, and advises the government on monetary policy. The bank was established under an Act of Parliament in July 1964 and started its operations in June, 1965 in Blantyre. RBM is a member of the Alliance for Financial Inclusion (AFI) and has been increasingly proactive in the implementation of various initiatives to combat the financial literacy issues that plague the nation. Its head office is currently at Convention Drive City Centre, Lilongwe.
BUSINESS SITUATION
RDB found that it suffers from infrastructural constraints and as the filing system was manual, with records maintained haphazardly and prone to decay. Search processes were inefficient, as either staff would not be available to retrieve the records or the record itself may have been misplaced.
RDB wanted to increase its efficiency by reducing the time it takes to register cases by 
automating the registry process, reduce time taken to retrieve file from the records and also reduce corruption by automating billing processes.
SOLUTION
Techno Brain BPO/ITES was approached by RBD to address their problem. Techno Brain proposed Scanning and Digitization of RDB’s records and storing them in document management system, centralized location enabling easy access of files. The purpose was to convert the physical records into electronic mode, which was to capture data, Document Scanning and Digitization Services of 6 million images at The Reserve Bank of Malawi within a realistic time-frame. The Lilongwe branch had 10 million + records for the, with the initial pilot project at only 45% of the volume i.e. 4.5 million records to be scanned and digitized. This phase 1 project was initiated 10th December 2013 and completed 18 April, 2014. The phase 2of the project at Blantyre branch was initiated on 22nd April 2014 and completed on 10 June, 2014.
BENEFITS: 
Scanning and Digitization of paper records in to electronic mode has been a huge success for RDB where they could experience a notable change in its day to day activities.SCANNING AND DIGITIZATION OF BANK RECORDS
CASE STUDY
CustomerThe Reserve Bank of Malawi is the central bank of Malawi whose main interest is maintaining a workable position, with respect to the balance of payments, attaining stability in local prices of items and achieving growth in real revenues. 
Industry
Public(Banking)
Business Situation
Manual creation of customer records, storing, searching and retrieval at RBM resulted in delayed treatment as records search & retrieval was time consuming, errors in details entry, duplication of records along with files misplacement also occured.
Solution
RBD had physical records converted into electronic mode, by capturing data, document Scanning and Digitization Services of 11.5 million images.
Benefits 
• Tracking of record, conversion of manual to electronic mode• To decrease the space constrain of records management• Being environment friendly, sustainability of all records• Improve quality of care for end user• Reduce budgetary constraints on procurements


Key benefits of Medical Records Scanning & Digitization:
• The new system led to a fast and speedy search and retrieval of records
• It helped in reduction of manual intervention during records retrieval process thereby mitigating risks such as records getting damaged, mixed up and duplication• The issue of space constraint in terms of storing the physical files was also resolved as everything is now stored digitally• Security enhanced with biometric access allowing authorized personnel to the records
